content.full_text:
August 8, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission  
Mr. John Rothmon  
Sacramento, California  
FAX: (916) 327-2026

Dear Mr. Rothmon:

Pursuant to our conversation, I am requesting an opinion from your office on the two situations I have discussed with you.

My name is Rick Jensen. I am presently serving as a member of the Madera County Board of Supervisors.

As I discussed with you, the property in question is owned by my father, Richard N. Jensen, Mildred Shelton, my aunt, and Shelley Bursey and Cina Garrison, my cousins, in a joint trust.

I am married and have no children. Neither my wife nor I have an interest in the above described property.

A group of property owners is attempting to form a new land use area. The proposed area consists of approximately 25,000 acres. The property my father, aunt, and two cousins own consists of approximately 1,400 acres and is within the Rio Mesa area (new land use area).

Do I have a "Conflict of Interest" as prescribed by the regulations that govern the Fair Political Practices Commission, if I participate in the discussion and/or vote concerning the establishment of the new land use area (Rio Mesa)?

Also, the aforementioned property owned by my father, aunt, and two cousins is part of a 5,000 acre site which is being considered by the University of California for a new campus.

FFPC Letter, 8/6/90
p. 2

Do I have a conflict of interest if I participate and/or vote on issues regarding the county's effort to bring a U.C. campus to Madera County?

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me at (209) 675-7700.

Sincerely,

Rick Jensen
